name: nacl_sealed_box
description: libsodium crypto_box_seal decrypt vectors. External implementations verify by decrypting these frozen ciphertexts with the recipient's private key and asserting plaintext matches. The ciphertext bytes are non-deterministic (each sealed-box call generates a fresh ephemeral X25519 keypair) — this vector freezes THESE particular ciphertexts produced once, at frozen_at time.

assertions.overhead_breakdown: 32-byte ephemeral X25519 public key || 16-byte Poly1305 MAC
assertions.ciphertext_layout: eph_pub (32) || encrypted_plaintext (N) || poly1305_mac (16) — total N + 48 bytes for N-byte plaintext

notes: The 'recipient.seed_hex' is `5a` repeated 32 times. crypto_box_seed_keypair(seed) deterministically derives an X25519 keypair: the public key is the Curve25519 scalar-mult of the seed-clamped private key against the base point.

The 'recipient.private_key_hex' IS shipped in this vector — deliberately. It's not a real key (5a-repeating seed is obviously a test fixture, never generated by CSPRNG); external implementations need it to exercise the decrypt path. DO NOT ever use this seed for production.

Ciphertext bytes are non-deterministic on produce because libsodium samples a fresh ephemeral X25519 keypair for each call. The recipient-side decrypt is fully deterministic: given the same {ciphertext, recipient_priv}, you always recover the same plaintext. This vector freezes the specific ciphertexts produced at frozen_at time.

An external implementation that produces its own ciphertext against the recipient_pub and successfully round-trips decrypt is considered L3-conformant, even though its ciphertext bytes won't match these vectors'.

The nonce formula (BLAKE2b-24 over ephemeral_pub || recipient_pub) is pinned separately in task 10.9 (blake2b_24_sealed_nonce.json) — this vector covers the overall sealed-box contract, not the nonce derivation specifically.

Tamper check: flipping any byte in the ciphertext MUST cause Poly1305 MAC verification to fail, and crypto_box_seal_open MUST return an error (not a garbled plaintext). The conformance test exercises this.
